title: Enemy
description: A combat adversary type (FR-CBT-02). Static definition only: a live fight's remaining hit points and alert state live in GameState.combat, seeded from this definition when an encounter begins. M1 shipped the lone walker; T46 adds the type-distinct roster (Fresh, Crawler, Bloated, Riot). Each keys to a zombie.<slug> behaviour def; combat picks the most dangerous type present at a node. The engine holds the authoritative combat dials as constants (a bridge, as with loot/walker stats); these files mirror them for the schema gate and a harness drift-guard.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema.

id.description: Stable content id of the form enemy.<slug>.

description.description: How the enemy reads in a combat Scene (ICU MessageFormat string).

maxHp.description: Hit points a fresh encounter with this enemy starts at.

armor.description: Flat melee damage mitigation (Riot, FR-CBT-07): each blunt strike is reduced by this much (floored at 0 net). A firearm punches through — armor never reduces a shot — so the right approach to an armored dead is a bullet or a wide berth. Defaults to 0.

burstInfection.description: Severity of the infectious wound the type inflicts on the killer when it dies at the player's node (Bloated, FR-INJ / FR-CBT-07): a bad thing to put down up close. 0 (default) means no burst — most types.

graspWound.description: Content id of the wound a fleeing player risks from this type on the way out (Crawler ankle-grab, FR-CBT-07): it makes slipping past costlier than past a plain walker. Absent means the type has no special parting wound.

initiative.description: Strikes back reliably — the sound of speed (Fresh, FR-CBT-07): a fast dead answers every melee exchange instead of only landing a blow half the time. Defaults to false.

notes.description: Author commentary (JSON has no comments, ADR-0002).
